content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

Accepted four amendments: three functional drift fixes and one comment-only correctness fix. Rejected one duplicate finding because it repeats the exact same stale `final_review_planner` snapshot issue as fr-1-3ea59dad.

## Votes

- `fr-1-3ea59dad`: Accept
  High-confidence regression: `StageResolutionSnapshot` no longer defines `final_review_planner`, yet the checked-in final-review snapshot still serializes it. Keeping this drift in fixture data misrepresents current model state, breaks strict deserialization assumptions, and can cause snapshot/e2e checks to drift or fail unpredictably.
- `fr-1-a85b0c74`: Accept
  High-confidence correctness issue: `final_review_reviewer_id()` is one-based (`reviewer-{index+1}`), while supporting record persistence uses zero-based suffixes for both proposal and vote artifacts. This inconsistent naming breaks direct ID->artifact correlation and makes operational debugging and downstream tooling brittle.
- `fr-1-fd259192`: Accept
  Valid maintainability bug: test names/comments still reference `planner` though assertions target arbiter behavior, which is misleading during triage and future edits. Renaming and updating comments removes ambiguity and prevents mistaken role assumptions.
- `fr-1-9ae1de46`: Accept
  The comment states the final-review panel excludes both planner/arbiter, but planner is no longer part of that role model. Updating this to exclude only arbiter aligns in-code documentation with real behavior and reduces confusion.
- `fr-1-202fdf64`: Reject
  This is a duplicate report of the same fixture field mismatch already captured by fr-1-3ea59dad. Addressing fr-1-3ea59dad resolves this concern; accepting both would create redundant duplicate work without additional technical gain.
